step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Although certain antiretroviral drugs require dose adjustment or avoidance in patients with renal impairment, modern guidelines emphasize that HIV itself is not an absolute contraindication to kidney transplantation. The Infectious Diseases Society of America explicitly supports evaluating HIV-positive patients with end-stage renal disease for transplantation, especially when they have well-controlled HIV and stable conditions through antiretroviral therapy. Therefore, exclusion of all such patients is inappropriate.
